intro: Antimima pygmaea, also known as the dwarf Antimima, is a small, succulent plant species endemic to the Western Cape province of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of A. pygmaea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
